subject: Accounting
title: Evergrande's Debt Bomb
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Evergrande's Debt Bomb.

hook.text: China Evergrande had $300 billion in liabilities. Its accounting made the situation look manageable.

buildup.text: The property developer booked revenue from apartments before they were built or delivered.

discovery.text: Off-balance-sheet vehicles and related-party transactions obscured the true scale of debt.

twist.text: When China tightened rules, Evergrande couldn't refinance. The dominoes started falling.

climax.text: The default threatened China's financial system and wiped out billions in investor wealth.

punchline.text: Hidden debt doesn't vanish. It compounds.

quiz.question: How did Evergrande obscure its debt?
options: Off-balance-sheet vehicles and early revenue recognition
